name: Pear Almond Tart
recipeInstructions:
Apricot preserves - 1/2 cup
Dark rum - 2 tablespoons
Almonds, finely ground - 1/2 cup
Granulated sugar - 1/2 cup
Flour - 1 tablespoon
Pears - 9 firm, ripe (preferably Anjou), peeled, halved lengthwise, and cored
To make pastry:
1. Combine dry ingredients and lemon peel in a food processor bowl fitted with a metal chopping blade.
2. Process just until mixed.
3. Add butter and cut into small pieces; process just until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
4. Add egg yolk; process just until blended.
5. With the processor running, add ice water quickly. Process several seconds until the pastry forms a ball.
6. Stop processing immediately to avoid overmixing.
7. Wrap the ball in plastic wrap and chill for at least one hour.
1. Butter a 10-inch quiche pan with a removable bottom.
2. Roll out the pastry slightly larger than the pan. Carefully place it onto the pan and gently press against the bottom and sides.
3. Trim the edges; prick the bottom and sides with a fork.
4. Chill for half an hour.
5. Line the pastry-filled pan with a circle of foil, pressing it gently against the pastry.
6. Fill the liner with uncooked rice or dried beans to weigh down. Bake in a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven for 15 minutes.
7. Remove the liner and uncooked filling; set aside for future use.
8. Bake the crust for an additional 5 minutes, then cool on a wire rack.
1. To make glaze, heat apricot preserves and rum over low heat, stirring. Strain and brush the tart shell with 2 tablespoons of glaze. Reserve the remainder.
2. Stir together almonds, sugar, and flour; sprinkle over the glazed shell.
3. Slice the pears crosswise (do not separate the slices). Place them in a circle over the almond mixture in the shell.
4. Chop the remaining pear halves and fill any spaces between the sliced pears.
5. Bake for 40 minutes in a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven.
6. Remove from the oven and brush with the remaining warm glaze.
Serve at room temperature.
